conditionDescriptions: Chronic kidney disease with eGFR < 30 ml/min/1.73m2
contraindications.clinicalRationale: Long-acting sulfonylureas or those cleared by the kidney should be avoided at low eGFRs due to impaired drug clearance and increased risk of prolonged hypoglycemia
contraindications.riskDescription: Increased risk of severe and prolonged hypoglycemia due to impaired drug clearance
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other glucose-lowering agents with lower hypoglycemia risk such as GLP-1 RA, DPP-4 inhibitors, or SGLT2 inhibitors
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Sulfonylureas that are long-acting or cleared by the kidney should be avoided at low eGFRs.

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Sulfonylureas should be discontinued in patients on dialysis due to inability to clear the drug and extremely high risk of severe hypoglycemia
contraindications.riskDescription: Severe and potentially life-threatening hypoglycemia due to drug accumulation
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Use insulin with careful monitoring or other dialysis-appropriate glucose-lowering agents
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Figure 23: Sulfonylurea — Discontinue when eGFR < 30; Dialysis: Discontinue.

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: High risk of hypoglycemia, especially in patients with advanced CKD
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor blood glucose closely, educate on hypoglycemia symptoms, consider dose reduction when used with other hypoglycemic agents

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Dose reduction may be needed when used concomitantly with GLP-1 receptor agonists or SGLT2 inhibitors
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients receiving combination therapy with GLP-1 RA or SGLT2 inhibitors
warnings.actionRequired: Consider sulfonylurea dose reduction when starting GLP-1 RA or SGLT2 inhibitors to prevent hypoglycemia

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Less suitable for patients requiring weight loss or those at high risk for hypoglycemia
warnings.affectedPopulation: Obese patients or those with history of hypoglycemia
warnings.actionRequired: Consider alternative glucose-lowering agents such as GLP-1 RA for patients needing weight loss or those at high hypoglycemia risk

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
